source: UEX Corp API (prices/shops) + Star Citizen Wiki API (mining specs/images)
attribution: Unofficial — not affiliated with Cloud Imperium Games. Prices are a community snapshot, not live. Data: UEX Corp + Star Citizen Wiki (CC-BY-SA).
note: Mining gear stats from game files via SC Wiki. Head passive modifiers parsed from localised descriptions; prices/locations are the cheaper of recent UEX reports.

heads.name: Arbor MHV Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Arbor has became the universe's most heralded mining laser thanks to Greycat Industrial's exacting standards and business prowess. The MHV takes that same classic design and reimagines it for a more compact mining-rig while still delivering the solid and reliable performance that miners have come to rely on.

heads.name: Lawson Mining Laser
heads.desc: The Lawson mining laser was developed by Argo Astronautics and specifically tuned for the ATLS GEO to make mining resources from the power suit as intuitive as possible.

heads.name: S0 Helix

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: Most powerful mining laser, strongest laser

heads.name: S00 Hofstede

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: This is the default mining head for the ROC DS slightly stronger than the Arbor

heads.name: Arbor MH1 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Arbor MH1 became the universe's most heralded mining laser thanks to Greycat Industrial's exacting standards and business prowess. The laser found favor among industrial and solo operations for balancing speed and safety while also featuring a special filtration system that reduces the amount of inert material collected. Greycat capitalized on this reputation by securing exclusive contracts that made the Arbor MH1 the standard laser head on most mining ships.

heads.name: Helix I Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: To some, the effective use of the Helix I has become a sign of a true mining professional. Thermyte Concern gave the laser impressive power that can bypass a deposit's resistance but also requires precision handling to hit the optimal charge window. Best deployed at close range, Helix I features two module slots so miners can tailor the laser to their liking.

heads.name: Hofstede-S1 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Hofstede-S1 was designed with a patented crystal lattice structure that dampens a deposit's natural resistance while mining and gathers less inert materials when extracting. Most effective at short and medium range, an accompanying increase in instability means the laser should be managed carefully.

heads.name: Impact I Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Impact I excels in a variety of mining situations. Special focus lenses make it easier to work a deposit by stabilizing the laser and increasing the optimal charge window. There are drawbacks, including increased deposit resistance and a slower filling optimal charge window, but Thermyte Concern also provided two module slots to allow miners to better customize the laser to the task at hand.

heads.name: Klein-S1 Mining Laser
heads.desc: Shubin designed the Klein-S1 to generate a powerful laser that makes mining faster. This power incurs additional risk by boosting a laser's instability, but if used correctly, the Klein-S1 can quickly reap rewards for the careful miner.

heads.name: Lancet MH1 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: Greycat classifies the Lancet MH1 as a support laser best utilized when mining with others. While the mining laser's small optimal charge window size means precision is key, the Lancet MH1 excels at reducing the amount of inert materials collected by the extraction laser. For mining crews, it's an ideal support laser to use alongside other lasers.

heads.name: Pitman Mining Laser
heads.desc: Bespoke to the Drake Golem, this mining laser was designed for those beginning a career in mining. It's been carefully calibrated to ensure an easy operator experience and reduce the likelihood of catastrophic accidents.

heads.name: Arbor MH2 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Arbor MH2 is the size two version of Greycat's heralded Arbor mining laser series. Like the smaller variant, the Arbor MH2's solid, reliable design found favor among industrial and solo operations for balancing speed and safety while also featuring a special filtration system that reduces the amount of inert material collected.

heads.name: Helix II Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: To some, the effective use of the Helix II has become a sign of a true mining professional. Thermyte Concern gave the laser impressive power that can bypass a deposit's resistance but also requires precision handling to hit the optimal charge window. Best deployed at close range, Helix II features three module slots so miners can tailor the laser to their liking.

heads.name: Hofstede-S2 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Hofstede-S2 was designed with a patented crystal lattice structure that dampens a deposit's natural resistance while mining and gathers less inert materials when extracting. Most effective at short and medium range, an accompanying increase in instability means the laser should be managed carefully.

heads.name: Impact II Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: The Impact II excels in a variety of mining situations. Special focus lenses make it easier to work a deposit by stabilizing the laser and increasing the optimal charge window. There are drawbacks, including increased deposit resistance and a slower filling optimal charge window, but Thermyte Concern also provided three module slots to allow miners to better customize the laser to the task at hand.

heads.name: Klein-S2 Mining Laser
heads.desc: Shubin designed the Klein-S2 to generate a powerful laser that makes mining faster. This power incurs additional risk by boosting a laser's instability, but if used correctly, the Klein-S2 can quickly reap rewards for the careful miner.

heads.name: Lancet MH2 Mining Laser

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

heads.desc: Greycat classifies the Lancet MH2 as a support laser best utilized when mining with others. While the mining laser's small optimal charge window size means precision is key, the Lancet MH2 excels at filling it once found and reducing the amount of inert materials collected by the extraction laser. For mining crews, it's an ideal support laser to use alongside other lasers.

modules.name: FLTR Module
modules.desc: Reduce the amount of inert material collected from deposits with the FLTR Module. Though it reduces the laser's extraction power, the FLTR's patented filtration system screens out inert material to leave more storage space for valuable ore.

modules.name: FLTR-L Module
modules.desc: Reduce the amount of inert material collected from deposits with the FLTR-L Module. Though it reduces the laser's extraction power, the FLTR-L's patented filtration system screens out inert material to leave more storage space for valuable ore.

modules.name: FLTR-XL Module
modules.desc: Reduce the amount of inert material collected from deposits with the FLTR-XL Module. Though it reduces the laser's extraction power, the FLTR-XL's patented filtration system screens out inert material to leave more storage space for valuable ore.

modules.name: Focus II Module
modules.desc: The Focus II Module uses a modified mining laser crystal to create a larger optimal charge window at the expense of laser power.

modules.name: Focus III Module
modules.desc: The Focus III Module uses a modified mining laser crystal to create a larger optimal charge window at the expense of laser power.

modules.name: Focus Module
modules.desc: The Focus Module uses a modified mining laser crystal to create a larger optimal charge window at the expense of laser power.

modules.name: ROC Module
modules.desc: This bespoke mining module was designed specifically for use with the ROC.

modules.name: Rieger Module
modules.desc: The Rieger Module boosts laser power but requires more precision to find an effective mining area.

modules.name: Rieger-C2 Module
modules.desc: The Rieger-C2 Module boosts laser power but requires more precision to find an effective mining area.

modules.name: Rieger-C3 Module
modules.desc: The Rieger-C3 Module boosts laser power but requires more precision to find an effective mining area.

modules.name: Torrent II Module
modules.desc: Save time while mining with the Torrent II Module. It requires steady hands to hit the sweet spot but accelerates the charge rate once there.

modules.name: Torrent III Module
modules.desc: Save time while mining with the Torrent III Module. It requires steady hands to hit the sweet spot but accelerates the charge rate once there.

modules.name: Torrent Module
modules.desc: Save time while mining with the Torrent Module. It requires steady hands to hit the sweet spot but accelerates the charge rate once there.

modules.name: Vaux Module
modules.desc: The Vaux Module from Shubin Interstellar reduces the charge rate when mining but makes extracting valuable ore faster.

modules.name: Vaux-C2 Module
modules.desc: The Vaux-C2 Module from Shubin Interstellar reduces the charge rate when mining but makes extracting valuable ore faster.

modules.name: Vaux-C3 Module
modules.desc: The Vaux-C3 Module from Shubin Interstellar reduces the charge rate when mining but makes extracting valuable ore faster.

modules.name: XTR Module
modules.desc: The XTR Module creates a wider mining window for deposits. It also makes the extraction laser less powerful so it can filter out more inert materials.

modules.name: XTR-L Module
modules.desc: The XTR-L Module creates a wider mining window for deposits. It also makes the extraction laser less powerful so it can filter out more inert materials.

modules.name: XTR-XL Module
modules.desc: The XTR-XL Module creates a wider mining window for deposits. It also makes the extraction laser less powerful so it can filter out more inert materials.

modules.name: Brandt Module

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

modules.desc: Strategically use the Brandt to boost a mining laser's power for sixty seconds. The modulated beam also has the benefit of helping make rocks that shatter less hazardous, though the deposit will be more resistant to the laser's affect overall.

modules.name: Forel Module
modules.desc: Shubin developed the Forel Module to provide a safer mining experience by significantly lowering the rate at which the catastrophic charge window fills. Though deposits will be more resistant, the module provides more power to the extraction laser to help it collect ore faster.

modules.name: Lifeline Module

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

modules.desc: The Lifeline makes a miner's work easier by reducing laser instability and lessening resistance within a deposit. Yet, a higher catastrophic charge rate means the process must be carefully managed to avoid an accident.

modules.name: Optimum Module
modules.desc: The Optimum makes mining safer by improving laser stability and dramatically decreasing the catastrophic charge rate. To achieve this increased safety, the module reduces the overall power of the mining laser.

modules.name: Rime Module
modules.desc: The Rime delivers a safer mining experience by sacrificing laser power to reduce a deposit's resistance and the dangers associated with shattering.

modules.name: Stampede Module
modules.desc: The Stampede uses a state of the art lens to increase mining laser power and reduce potential hazard associated with the deposit shattering. These improvements do come at the expense of a less powerful extraction laser.

modules.name: Surge Module
modules.desc: Instantly inject energy into a deposit with the Surge. It uses a special configuration of lenses to increase mining laser power and bypass a deposit's resistance. It does require careful management as the module also increases laser instability.

modules.name: Torpid Module
modules.desc: The Torpid Module is a must for any ambitious miner. It makes mining more efficient by dramatically increasing the optimal charge rate while also reducing the catastrophic charge rate. The module does have a downside as a shattering deposit may prove more hazardous.

gadgets.name: BoreMax

locations.terminal: Tools & Supplies - People's Service Station Alpha
Tools & Supplies - People's Service Station Theta
Tools & Supplies - People's Service Station Lambda
Tools & Supplies - People's Service Station Delta

gadgets.desc:
The BoreMax helps focus laser power to reduce instability significantly, while also keeping clusters of valuable deposits together upon shattering. While it does make it more difficult and time-consuming to bypass the deposit's resistance, most miners find the extra safety and increased yield worth the extra effort.

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.

gadgets.name: Okunis
gadgets.desc:
Increase the size and fill speed of the optimal charge window with the Okunis. While this powerful gadget makes it easier to work a deposit, some valuable resource will be lost when it shatters. 

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.

gadgets.name: OptiMax
gadgets.desc:
Commonly found on industrial sites across the empire, the OptiMax reduces the loss of valuable resource through a special process that keeps them clustered together. This technology does impose drawbacks on the gadget though, most notably a reduced optimal charge window. 

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.

gadgets.name: Sabir

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

gadgets.desc:
Make that tough deposit easier to crack with the Sabir. It uses a special sensor to modulate laser power and adjust it to that particular deposit. The effect increases instability but also provides a dramatic drop in resistance and an increased optimal charge window to make mining it easier. 

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.

gadgets.name: Stalwart
gadgets.desc:
The Stalwart is for miners looking to wield power precisely. The gadget reduces the optimal charge window size, making miners skillfully manage conditions to find the sweet spot. Once there, the increased power will fill the optimal charge window faster while also keeping clusters of valuable ore together. 

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.

gadgets.name: Waveshift

locations.terminal: Tammany and Sons - Metro Center - Lorville

gadgets.desc:
A unique chipset allows the WaveShift to keep instability in check while dramatically increasing the size of the optimal charge window. Though the optimal charge window will fill slower, the larger target area makes it easier to find the perfect spot to shatter a deposit. 

Manufacturer Warning! Using more than one mining gadget per deposit can result in a catastrophic explosion.
